Key Takeaways:
• Example 2: “Go Romance” - This example a bit more complex
• Timing
o 9 emails - plus the blog and a landing page
o 2 weeks
• Offer
o Could be a bundle of digital products
o Discount off normal price - ex: $350 value for $97
o In actuality, by bundling it ends up being the highest price ever for this website in
the example
• Triggers
o Reason why
o Story
o Scarcity
o Event-based
o Community-based
o Likeability
o Conversation
o Anticipation
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 10 --

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
o Emotion
• Email 1
o Triggers: story, likability (shades of reluctant hero)
o My client was sharing a personal story about daughter getting married and wrote
that she was nervous about sharing - this instantly brings people in
o “Just press the reply button in your email right now” - this gets a much bigger
response than you can even imagine
o The email tells a story and creates a lot of likeability
• Email 2
o Triggers: story, community, likeability, reciprocity, conversation, social proof
o Email begins with gratitude for the 200 email responses from email 1 creates
conversation and builds the community
• Email 3
o Triggers: story, likeability, reciprocity, community, conversation
o Lots of consistent triggers but the entire thing is very event based
o Sends people to a blog - this creates huge social proof
o People see all the notes with all the love in them - ended up with 4-500 notes
• Email 4
o Triggers: story, reason why, community, conversation
o Brings it all full circle - is sent after the bridal shower
o Switches the theme and moves into a “thank you plan”
o Offers a huge special discount on the products - created an entire bundle that didn’t
exist before
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 10 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
o This email is the switch to the offer
• Email 5
o Triggers: story, reason why, community, conversation, scarcity, anticipation
o Adds in a little reluctant hero, “took a little longer than I expected to put this
together”
o “Putting finishing touches on the offer”
o Special bonuses for the first 50 people - first time scarcity is brought in
• Email 6
o Triggers: scarcity
o Announcement email: “Mother of all offers is ready” - go check it out right away
o Use early bird bonuses
o Tip: When you’re going live with something you’ve spent a lot of time building up
to, make the email short and get them straight to the order page
• Email 7
o Triggers: story, strong reason why, scarcity
o Refers back to the “thank you plan”
o Explains a reason why for the deadline (scarcity) - needs to close down before the
wedding
• Email 8
o Triggers: conversation, reason why, scarcity, social proof
o Reason why message - offers a FAQ
o Take the top objections to what you’re selling and answer them
o This can be done through email, blog post, or video

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
o In the example, she answered the three primary questions
• Email 9
o End of the launch - 24-hour warning
o Always a big spike at the end so don’t forget to end with a warning
o Lots of scarcity and strong reason why
Summary:
• Use the “Go Romance” technique to tell a story and sell one or more products
• This technique creates a lot of emotion and builds community, social proof, and fan love
• Many different triggers can be used
• The launch is longer and more involved but delivers massive results
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 10 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Next up we have the Go Romance sale.
This one is a little different than the tax sale.
The timing on this one is a more involved Quick Launch. There were 10 emails,
the entire thing took about two weeks and had the 10 emails, plus the blog, plus the
landing page.
The offer was a bundle of digital products that had never been bundled before
this promotion. The offer was a discount off the normal price.
If you took the pricing of the entire bundle together, I can’t remember how much
it cost, but I think it was probably a $300 value, and I think the discount price was $97.
Something like that.
Even though it was a big discount for the bundle price, it was the highest price
ever for this website. They never had been anywhere near this price point. The result was
they ended up having this launch, which just lasted a few days, doing far better than they
had ever done in an entire month before. That was the power of this Quick Launch.
The triggers – there’s a lot of them. Over the 10 days they had more time to
develop the triggers. They had a strong reason why and they had a very strong story.
They used scarcity. It was event based, community based, a lot of likability here. There
was a real conversation going on, some anticipation, and a bunch of emotion.
Let’s take a look at the way these all played out.
This is the very first email. This is a romance site; it was a site mostly about
writing love letters. The beautiful thing here was that my client Leslie, her daughter was
getting married. I said, “This is the absolute best ever reason for a launch.” The entire
story of the launch was based around this upcoming wedding.
It starts right off with a little bit of the reluctant hero, which is something we’re
going to be talking about throughout the course. This is a very attractive trait. When you
come out and say you’re nervous about something and you say that in public, people just
rally to you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 10 --

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Its comes out, “I’ve got some exciting personal news and I’m a little bit nervous
about sharing it. Even though I’m normally out there with my public personality, I
usually talk about my private life very much, which is why I’m a bit nervous.” It’s
reluctant hero and it really pulls people in.
“I’m bursting with this news. My daughter is getting married.”
I actually wrote this copy – this is the first time I ever wrote copy in a woman’s
voice. I had this note in to Leslie, my client, telling her where to fill in the blanks.
Down here this is real personable stuff, this is the likability. Letting people into
your life. “I give the world advice on romance, but normally I don’t feel like I can give my
daughter lessons. I’m looking to you to help.” That’s really what the story is behind here.
“I have a simple favor to ask.”
It’s funny, because when you ask a favor it almost brings in the reciprocity. It
seems backwards that you’re asking them to give you something, but when you ask a
favor in this manner it actually brings in the reciprocity and people feel like they owe
you, because you’re just asking in this very personal way. It’s very interesting psychology.
She wants to shower her daughter and daughter’s fiancé with well wishes and
love, so she asks, “Simply type in a simple message to the newlyweds to be and hit the
send button. I’m hoping to get hundreds or thousands of responses. We’re going to put
the responses together and give them to Abby and Jonathon, the happy couple. Just
press the reply button in your email program.”
These are magic words right here. If you want a response, if you put those words,
“Just press the reply button in your email program right now and send it to me,” that will
get a bigger response than anything you could ever imagine.
Here she’s just thanking them for their time. This is a very likable message. In
real life Leslie is very likeable, but in this email she is extremely likable.
Now in email number two we’re hitting the story again, community, likability, the
reciprocity, and conversation. This is basically a reaction the very next day, it’s an email
about the reaction that came in. It really was an amazing reaction, there was over 200
emails back giving heartfelt wishes, well wishes to Abby and Jonathon as they were about
to get married.
That’s the community and the conversation. In this email Leslie is responding to
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 10 --

8
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
the emails she got, so that instantly is building the community. Even though these people
that are responding and reading this can’t see that community, this is building
community when you start to talk about it. It’s also building social proof. There’s social
proof when you talk about the 200 emails that you got.
Even though there’s no call to action, there’s nothing to do, it’s very strong
because it has all these triggers. It has the story, it has the community, people writing
back, it has the conversation. You might not see a conversation, but basically Leslie is
talking about these emails she got back and that she is actually reading these emails.
She’s talking about her heart overflowing, her response to the emails.
She’s talking about getting these emails and reading these emails, and reacting to
the emails, so there is conversation built into this email.
This next email is where the magic really starts to kick in. Again we’re hitting
story, likability, the reciprocity, community, conversation. We keep coming back to these
same triggers. Later on we’ll add in scarcity. That’s the beautiful thing, it’s all even based
around this wedding shower.
The wedding shower is coming up, but now Leslie has decided there’s too much
love and caring in these notes to keep them in the family, so she set up a blog and people
can go look at the pages and pages. The beautiful thing here is it’s huge social proof as
they go to this blog.
This blog is not up for you to look at anymore, but it was impossible to not be
moved when you went to this blog, because there were so many notes with so much love
in them. It was crazy, I think it ended up being 400 to 500 notes in total. People could
come to this blog, look at all of those, and then add in their notes on top of it. It was just
incredibly wonderfully warm and fuzzy. It was just beautiful. This all of a sudden started
to bring real emotion into this entire story. It was almost impossible not to get emotional
when you saw all the posts on this blog.
This fourth email just brings it full circle. This comes out after the shower. You
see the beauty of this is you’re bringing them into your life and you’re walking them
through this even that you had. Now you’re talking about all the notes that they had,
printing them out, giving them to Abby and Jonathon, and the beautiful shower.
Now, here’s a little bit of a switch. Now is where the marketing comes in. “I’ve
reflected over the last few days and I want to thank you.” Basically the thank you plan is
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 10 --

9
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
to put together a huge special discount on the products. What we’re doing, as I
mentioned earlier, it is a huge discount, but it’s a huge discount over the entire bundle.
Normally no one ever bought the bundle. In fact, there wasn’t a bundle available,
this was the first time. She basically built this mother of the bride bundle with the
mother of the bride discount. That’s why even in whole it’s a discount over the whole
bundle, it’s still the highest price ever that Leslie had on her website. This email is the
switch to the pitch, that the pitch is coming.
Next email, number five. This basically just refers. Here we’re hitting story and
reason why, scarcity, conversation, community, and now anticipation is added into the
mix, referring back to the whole story here.
“It took a little longer than expected,” a little of the reluctant hero there.
“Here’s the mother of all offers. It’s coming up tomorrow.” This is where we start
to build anticipation.
“Putting the finishing touches on it.” This is bringing them in, it’s not I’m putting
together some big marketing pitch for you, I’m just putting the finishing touches on this
mother of all offers.
“My apologies,” this is sort of the aw shucks, apologies.
“One other thing. There’s special bonuses for the first 50 people.” This is the first
time that we’ve brought scarcity into the mix.
This is the announcement email, “The mother of all offers is ready. Go check it
out right away.” And there’s early bird bonuses. That’s the only real trigger we’re hitting
here is scarcity. Very short and simple.
When you’re going live with something that you’ve built up to, you don’t want to
spend a lot of time on copy, you just want to get right to it and get them on the order
page or the sales letter.
The next one a couple days later, this is just referring back to the thank you plan.
“Unfortunately, our problem’s getting the email out. Best laid plans.” This is just story,
strong reason why, and scarcity.
Here is where some of the magic takes place. We have scarcity, time is drawing
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 10 --

10
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
tight, but we have a reason why for the deadline. It’s because Leslie wants to close this
thing down before the wedding, because she has lots on her mind.
Down here reiterates sort of the story and the offer, the reason why. Here we
reiterate scarcity with the first 50. This is the first hint that the offer is coming down, that
it’s a very limited offer in terms of timing, and it’s because the wedding is on Saturday.
Next up we’re hitting all kinds of triggers here; conversation, reason why,
scarcity, and social proof. This is an FAQ message. This can be a really nice thing to drop
in the middle of a launch. If you have a launch where it’s open for several days and you’re
wondering what to mail on the second or third day, you an FAQ (frequently asked
questions).
Basically this is where you take the top objections to whatever you’re selling and
then you answer them. You can do it in an email like this, you could do it in a blog post,
or you could do it in a video.
This was a low tech Quick Launch, so we just went with email. You can see we
answered three primary objections. “Does this stuff really work? Shouldn’t a relationship
come naturally? Is it okay to use someone else’s love letters?” These are actually
objections that we received. “I’m not in long distance relationship,” because one of the
ebooks was about long distance relationships.
Then this is just the end of the launch, the closing down in 24 hours warning. You
always get a big spike at the end, so don’t forget to end with the 24-hour warning. Strong
reason why, scarcity, scarcity, scarcity. Very strong scarcity here.
The one thing I will tell you right here, I screwed up, it was only nine emails. Let’s
just go ahead and edit that – sorry about that, I screwed up.
That was the Quick Launch for Go Romance. Best results ever. They did better in
those few days that they were open than they had ever done in an entire month. It was a
little bit more involved Quick Launch, but basically just a series of emails and a special
offer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
